name: Urdax-Muga Lepo – circular por la Ruta Transpirenaica
region_intro: Hiking around Urdazubi/Urdax offers diverse terrain within the Pyrenees mountains in Navarre. The region features green meadows, dense forests, and mountains of medium height. The Ugarana River flows through the area, contributing to the varied landscape. This location provides a range of natural features suitable for different hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Urdazubi/Urdax The most popular hiking route is Cave of Zugarramurdi – circular from Urdazubi/Urdax, a 6.6 miles (10.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 19 minutes to complete. This moderate route links Urdazubi/Urdax with the Zugarramurdi area. Another top favourite among local hikers is Baztan Way (Camino de Santiago): Urdax to Ainhoa, a moderate 7.2 miles (11.6 km) path. This section of the Camino de Santiago offers scenic Basque landscapes. Local hikers also love the Monasterio de San Salvador loop from Urdazubi/Urdax, a 6.7 miles (10.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 26 minutes. Hiking around Urdazubi/Urdax is defined by Pyrenees mountains, green meadows, and forests, with routes offering options for different ability levels. The routes in Urdazubi/Urdax are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 270 reviews. More than 1900 hikers have used komoot to explore Urdazubi/Urdax's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find in Urdazubi/Urdax?
A: Urdazubi/Urdax offers a diverse range of hiking trails, from easy walks through green meadows and forests to more challenging routes in the Pyrenees mountains. There are 23 routes documented, with 2 easy, 17 moderate, and 4 difficult options, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Urdazubi/Urdax?
A: Yes, the region has trails suitable for families. The &lt;em&gt;Sentier de la Forge (Forge Trail)&lt;/em&gt; is a good example, offering a pleasant walk along a river and through a beautiful forest. While not explicitly listed in the guide data, the general terrain of green meadows and forests often provides accessible paths for all ages.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes around Urdazubi/Urdax?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968989232/cave-of-zugarramurdi-circular-from-urdazubi-urdax" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of Zugarramurdi – circular from Urdazubi/Urdax&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 10.6 km trail taking about 3 hours and 19 minutes.

Q: What natural landmarks or points of interest can I see while hiking?
A: Urdazubi/Urdax is rich in natural and historical landmarks. You can explore the impressive Urdax Caves (Ikaburu Caves) with their unique geological formations. Trails also lead through ancient oak groves, and some offer views of the Otsondo Pass. For panoramic views, consider routes that pass near peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1016283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rhune summit&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1365446" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont Artzamendi&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region is steeped in history. The Urdax Caves have significant archaeological findings from the Paleolithic period. You can also hike a section of the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968973474/baztan-way-camino-de-santiago-urdax-to-ainhoa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baztan Way (Camino de Santiago)&lt;/a&gt;, which passes through Urdazubi/Urdax. Another route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10517791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de San Salvador loop from Urdazubi/Urdax&lt;/a&gt;, takes you past the historic Monastery of San Salvador, dating back to the 9th century.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Urdazubi/Urdax?
A: The Pyrenees region generally offers pleasant hiking conditions from spring through autumn. Spring brings lush greenery and wildflowers, while autumn showcases vibrant foliage. Summer is also popular, though some higher elevation routes might be warmer. Winter can offer unique snowy landscapes, but requires appropriate gear and caution.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Urdazubi/Urdax dog-friendly?
A: Many trails in the Urdazubi/Urdax area are suitable for hiking with dogs, especially those through forests and meadows. It's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, particularly in areas with livestock or protected wildlife, and to check local regulations for specific trails or natural parks you plan to visit.

Q: How long do hikes in Urdazubi/Urdax typically take?
A: Hike durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. For example, a moderate route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968989232/cave-of-zugarramurdi-circular-from-urdazubi-urdax" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of Zugarramurdi – circular from Urdazubi/Urdax&lt;/a&gt; takes about 3 hours and 19 minutes for 10.6 km. More challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1286780" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ridge line – Zuharreta Pastures loop from Ainhoa&lt;/a&gt;, can take over 6 hours for 18 km.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Urdazubi/Urdax?
A: The hiking routes in Urdazubi/Urdax are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 270 reviews. Hikers often praise the scenic Basque landscapes, the variety of terrain from river valleys to mountain passes, and the well-maintained paths that allow for immersive outdoor experiences.

Q: Is parking available near the trailheads in Urdazubi/Urdax?
A: While specific parking locations are not detailed for every trailhead, Urdazubi/Urdax is a small town, and parking is generally available in or near the village center, which often serves as a starting point for many routes. For popular attractions like the Urdax Caves, dedicated parking facilities are typically provided.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, there are more difficult routes available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1286780" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ridge line – Zuharreta Pastures loop from Ainhoa&lt;/a&gt;, which is an 18 km hike with significant elevation gain, typically taking over 6 hours to complete. These routes often lead to higher elevations and offer expansive views of the Pyrenees.
tips: Beautiful views from the trans-Pyrenees route in the section that runs between Roncesvalles and Hondarribia. There will be some sections with a lot of slope and others where the path becomes somewhat technical but go firmly and carefully making sure you enjoy the beautiful landscape!

If you appreciate the panoramic views but don't want to sacrifice so much to get to the Col de Gorospil, this is undoubtedly a nice place to take a break and appreciate those wonderful views. From here you can continue uphill to Col de Gorospil, although the path itself is quite demanding!
Entertaining path with good cyclability through the forest and with impressive views of the entire valley of the protected area of Orabidea-Arxuri.
